rules.title: Missing Witness Schedule
rules.description: Chargesheet must contain a witness schedule listing all witnesses with their bayan reference numbers

remediation.summary: Add a complete witness schedule to the chargesheet
steps: List all witnesses examined under CrPC 161/BNSS 180
Include bayan statement reference numbers
Specify whether each witness is eye-witness, expert, or formal
Ensure complainant is listed as a witness

rules.title: Missing Seizure Memo Reference
rules.description: Chargesheet should reference seizure memos for all physical evidence collected

remediation.summary: Include seizure memo references in evidence schedule
steps: List all items seized during investigation
Reference each seizure panchnama by number and date
Ensure chain of custody is documented

rules.title: Missing Accused Particulars
rules.description: Full particulars of all accused persons must be included: name, parentage, age, address, identification marks

steps: Include full name with father/husband name
Record age and date of birth
Provide complete residential address
Note identification marks
Specify role in the offence

rules.title: Missing IO Identification
rules.description: Investigating Officer details (name, rank, belt number) must be clearly stated

steps: State IO full name and rank
Include belt/buckle number
Mention date of taking over investigation
Reference any transfer of investigation orders

rules.title: Missing Court Jurisdiction Statement
rules.description: Chargesheet must state the court of competent jurisdiction where the case is being filed

steps: Identify court of competent jurisdiction based on offence severity
For sessions-triable offences, file before Magistrate for committal
State full court name and address

rules.title: Malformed FIR Reference
rules.description: Chargesheet must contain a valid FIR reference number linking to the original complaint

remediation.summary: Ensure valid FIR reference in chargesheet
steps: Include FIR number, date, and police station
Verify FIR number matches the registered case
Cross-reference with CCTNS records

rules.title: Missing Charges/Sections
rules.description: Chargesheet must list the specific legal sections under which the accused is being charged

remediation.summary: List all applicable legal sections with charges
steps: Include primary offence sections (BNS/IPC)
Add companion sections where applicable
Specify which sections apply to which accused
Include special act provisions if relevant

rules.title: Missing Evidence Schedule
rules.description: Chargesheet should contain an evidence schedule listing all documentary and material evidence

steps: List all documentary evidence with serial numbers
Reference each material object with seizure memo
Include expert reports with dates
Note status of each evidence item (collected/pending/sent to FSL)

rules.title: Missing Filing Date
rules.description: Chargesheet must have a valid filing date within statutory time limits

steps: Record the date of filing before the court
Verify filing is within statutory time limit (60/90 days per CrPC 167/BNSS 187)
If time limit exceeded, note reasons and any extension orders

rules.title: Missing Disposal of Accused Statement
rules.description: Chargesheet should state the current disposal status of each accused (arrested/on bail/absconding)

steps: State whether each accused is in judicial custody or on bail
For absconding accused, mention non-bailable warrant details
Reference bail order numbers where applicable
